title: How Democrats Can Win, According to This Nobel Economist
description: An expert in human behavior explains how Democrats can change their primary process to improve their chances in 2028.
author: Richard Thaler

title: Clarence B. Jones, Who Helped Shape ‘I Have a Dream’ Speech, Dies at 95
description: A confidant and lawyer for Martin Luther King, he was an unseen hand behind major civil rights events, including the 1963 March on Washington.
author: Robert D. McFadden

title: As A.I. Fever Rises in Silicon Valley, Pope Leo Has a Few Words
description: The American pope wants to take artificial intelligence down a notch. Is he challenging the tech companies, or will tech take over the papacy?
author: David Streitfeld

title: A Terrible Winter for Snow Heads Into a Bleak Summer of Drought
description: In the Desert and Mountain West, towns like Kearny, Ariz., are already warning residents they could soon run out of water, but rationing so far has been patchy.
author: Jack Healy, Reis Thebault and Scott Dance

title: To Get the Strait Open, Trump Had to Leave the Hardest Issues for Later
description: President Trump is hailing the agreement with Iran as groundbreaking, even as he admits it “isn’t even fully negotiated.” But the nuclear stockpile, enrichment and missiles have not been discussed.
author: David E. Sanger and Tyler Pager

title: How to Be Old
description: My advice for how to keep living.
author: Roger Rosenblatt

title: A Poem for Memorial Day
description: We remember those who gave their lives in service to the United States.
author: Sam Sifton

title: One-and-Done Heart Disease Prevention? Scientists Show It May Be Possible.
description: A single infusion of an experimental gene-editing drug seemed to reduce LDL long-term in a small trial. The results may point to something “curative,” one expert said.
author: Gina Kolata

title: One of Madagascar’s Oldest Sacred Trees Is Slowly Dying
description: In a forest in Madagascar, the demise of a centuries-old baobab points to the fraying of a fragile ecosystem.
author: Jonathan Wolfe

title: Trump’s Self-Indulgent Streak Deepens G.O.P. Fears He Is Risking Losses in Midterms
description: “The stupid stuff is killing our chances,” said a retiring Republican senator.
author: Shane Goldmacher and Patricia Mazzei

title: Twenty Years After His Film, Al Gore Tweaks the Climate Script
description: Mr. Gore is still giving the slide show that “An Inconvenient Truth” was built around, but with changes that reflect a shift in the discussion of climate change.
author: Chico Harlan

title: Confusion and Worry After Trump Administration’s Abrupt Green Card Changes
description: Immigrants and their advocates and lawyers are trying to interpret a new Trump administration rule that requires people to be in their native country to apply for a green card.
author: Christina Morales

title: My Wife Fought Corruption in El Salvador. Then She Vanished Into Its Prisons.
description: The cost of Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on crime is too high.
author: Louis Benavides

title: With Big Decisions Ahead, the Supreme Court Collides With a Testy Trump
description: President Trump has alternated between bullying the justices and cozying up to them as the court prepares to announce major decisions that will determine the fate of the key aspects of his agenda.
author: Ann E. Marimow

title: At an LA Costco, Skateboarding and Learning About Loss
description: We’re all going to die. But first — just one more trick.
author: Conor Dougherty and Jake Michaels

title: How Prediction Markets and Crypto Firms Steamrolled a Watchdog Agency
description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission purged its ranks, dialed back its enforcement and boosted industries in which President Trump’s family is heavily invested.
author: Sharon LaFraniere and David Yaffe-Bellany

title: The 15-Year-Old Keeping War Memories Alive
description: Luke Morrison may be the youngest person keeping alive an age-old tradition: to process war through the memories and mementos of those who experienced it.
author: Jasper Craven and Amina Gingold

title: Colbert’s Sudden Return to Late Night? It Happened on Public Access.
description: A day after the comedian hosted the final episode of “The Late Show With Stephen Colbert” on CBS, he hosted “Only in Monroe,” a community-access program in Monroe, Mich.
author: Derrick Bryson Taylor

title: Short Naps, Long Hours: How Autism Clinics Squeeze Medicaid Dollars Out of Preschoolers
description: The industry has grown rapidly, straining state budgets. A focus on finances has led to overbilling, fraud and even harm.
author: Sarah Kliff, Margot Sanger-Katz, Erin Schaff and Asmaa Elkeurti

title: HUD Moves to Limit Assistance Animals for Disabled Tenants
description: Housing officials were told to exclude emotional support animals and tighten the definition of a service animal when granting accommodations for disabled tenants.
author: Chris Cameron and Debra Kamin
